skill.name: hft-quant-expert
skill.description: Guides quantitative trading strategy design, signal review, position sizing, and backtest checks for DeFi and crypto derivatives workflows.
skill.summary: Review crypto quant strategies, risk controls, backtests, and trading signals.
skill.author: BarisSozen

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command execution at SKILL.md:39 and weak cryptography at SKILL.md:3. Both are false positives: the first is a fenced Python formula block, and the second refers to crypto derivatives, not cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:39 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The location is the start of a fenced Python example for trading formulas, with no shell execution instruction or command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are a Markdown Python code block containing formula examples. No evidence found of backticks used for command execution or user-controlled shell input.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:3 for weak cryptography. The line describes crypto derivatives as a trading domain and does not define or use a cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The term crypto is used in the financial asset sense. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any other weak cryptographic primitive.

content.value_statement: Crypto trading strategies often fail from weak signals, biased tests, or unmanaged costs. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code review quant workflows before implementation.
actual_capabilities: Explains z-score, Sharpe ratio, Kelly sizing, and mean reversion half-life formulas.
Guides entry signal definition for quantitative trading strategies.
Reviews backtest design for lookahead bias, survivorship bias, and overfitting.
Highlights gas, slippage, and transaction costs in DeFi profit calculations.
Supports risk management discussion for crypto derivatives and DeFi strategies.
limitations: Does not execute trades, connect to exchanges, or manage live capital.
Does not provide guaranteed investment returns or personalized financial advice.
Does not include market data feeds, backtest engines, or exchange integrations.
Requires users to validate assumptions with current market data and compliance rules.

use_cases.title: Review a Mean Reversion Signal
use_cases.description: Check whether a z-score strategy has clear entries, exits, and cost assumptions before coding.

use_cases.title: Assess Backtest Quality
use_cases.description: Identify common testing flaws such as lookahead bias, survivorship bias, and overfitting in a proposed study.

use_cases.title: Set Risk Controls
use_cases.description: Translate win rate, payout ratio, volatility, and drawdown limits into practical position sizing guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Define a Basic Signal
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define a simple z-score trading signal for a crypto pair. Include entry rules, exit rules, and required data.

prompt_templates.title: Check Backtest Bias
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my backtest plan for lookahead bias, survivorship bias, overfitting, and missing DeFi transaction costs.

prompt_templates.title: Size a Position
prompt_templates.prompt: Given win probability, payout ratio, volatility, and maximum drawdown, propose a conservative position sizing approach.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Full Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this crypto derivatives strategy from signal design through execution costs, risk controls, and performance metrics.

output_examples.input: Review a z-score mean reversion idea for ETH perpetual futures.
output_examples.output: The response would outline signal construction, rolling window selection, entry and exit thresholds, slippage assumptions, funding costs, and bias checks.

output_examples.input: Help me choose a position size for a strategy with a small edge.
output_examples.output: The response would compare full Kelly and fractional Kelly sizing, explain drawdown risk, and suggest conservative caps for volatile markets.

output_examples.input: Find weaknesses in my backtest design.
output_examples.output: The response would flag data leakage, future information use, unrealistic fills, omitted gas costs, and excessive parameter tuning.

best_practices: Provide asset universe, timeframe, fee model, slippage model, and data source before asking for review.
Separate signal research, execution assumptions, and risk limits so each part can be tested.
Validate every result out of sample and include realistic DeFi costs before trusting performance.
anti_patterns: Treating a high Sharpe ratio as reliable without checking sample size and bias.
Ignoring gas, funding, slippage, liquidation risk, or failed transaction costs.
Changing many parameters until the backtest looks profitable.

faq.question: Does this skill trade automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides analysis guidance and review support, but it does not connect to exchanges or execute orders.

faq.question: Can it provide financial advice?
faq.answer: No. It supports research workflows and risk review, but users must make independent decisions and follow applicable rules.

faq.question: What markets does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on DeFi and crypto derivatives, especially strategy signals, backtests, volatility, and position sizing.

faq.question: Does it include a backtesting engine?
faq.answer: No. It explains how to evaluate backtests, but it does not ship software or market data.

faq.question: What inputs improve the output?
faq.answer: Use clear assumptions for timeframe, assets, fees, slippage, fills, leverage, and risk limits.

faq.question: Can it review execution costs?
faq.answer: Yes. It explicitly reminds users to include gas, slippage, funding, and other transaction costs.
